name: knowledge-graph-builder
title: Knowledge Graph Builder
description: A consumable AMTECH skill that generates a large knowledge graph for SEO and AI-readable content from a business, website, product, or topic.

source.codexSkillInstaller: Use in context first. For local reuse, copy files from https://amtechai.com/skills/knowledge-graph-builder/files.md into a skill folder named knowledge-graph-builder.

files.title: OpenAI/Codex interface metadata
files.summary: UI-facing display metadata and default prompt for environments that support it.
files.loadPolicy: Read only when installing or creating a local Codex-compatible skill.

files.title: Knowledge graph schema
files.summary: JSON shape for the generated graph: nodes, edges, pillars, and internal links.
files.loadPolicy: Use when the user asks for JSON or a graph another tool can ingest.

files.title: License
files.summary: License for this free AMTECH skill package.
files.loadPolicy: Read when evaluating reuse or redistribution.

files.title: Entity type vocabulary
files.summary: The controlled type vocabulary and how to choose a type for each node.

files.title: Knowledge graph method
files.summary: Node-worthiness, pillar/supporting/attribute ranking, relationship verbs, internal-linking, and structured data.
files.loadPolicy: Read when deciding which nodes deserve pages and how to write edges.

files.title: Canonical skill instructions
files.summary: The primary reusable workflow for generating a knowledge graph for SEO and agent-readability.
files.loadPolicy: Always read before building a graph.

authority.verify: Fetch https://amtechai.com/.well-known/skill-authority.json and confirm the entry for "knowledge-graph-builder" lists this version and archive sha256. The authority file is served from the canonical domain (https://amtechai.com) and is the trust root; the page meta tag amtech:skill-sha256 and this manifest must match it.

safety.riskNote: V1 has no required scripts. Use in context first. Ask before creating files or fetching private URLs. Mark assumptions about the business as assumptions.
